<script lang="ts">
	interface Props {
		active?: boolean;
		onclick: (e: MouseEvent) => void;
		label: string;
		testid?: string;
		ariaPressed?: boolean;
		clear?: boolean;
	}
	let { active = false, onclick, label, testid, ariaPressed, clear = false }: Props = $props();

	const activeCls =
		'bg-accent-lever text-white dark:bg-dark-accent-lever';
	const inactiveCls =
		'bg-bg-surface text-fg-muted hover:bg-bg-elevated hover:text-fg-ink dark:bg-dark-bg-surface dark:text-dark-fg-muted dark:hover:bg-dark-bg-elevated dark:hover:text-dark-fg-ink';
	const clearCls =
		'text-fg-muted hover:text-fg-ink dark:text-dark-fg-muted dark:hover:text-dark-fg-ink';
</script>

<button
	type="button"
	data-testid={testid}
	aria-pressed={ariaPressed}
	{onclick}
	class="rounded-full px-3 py-1 font-label-caps text-label-caps uppercase transition-colors {clear
		? clearCls
		: active
			? activeCls
			: inactiveCls}"
>
	{label}
</button>

data.text:
Committed.

**AC verification:**
1. **Runs page imports chip primitives from artifacts (extracted as shared)** — `FilterChip.svelte` in `lib/components/`; runs/+page.svelte:10 imports it; artifacts/+page.svelte:6 also imports it.
2. **Virtualization kicks in past threshold** — runs/+page.svelte: when `edges.length >= 1000`, each `<tr>` gets `content-visibility: auto; contain-intrinsic-size: 0 49px;`, and the wrapper exposes `data-virtualized="true"`.
3. **Live worker phase updates still work through virtualized list** — DOM semantics, row expansion (`RunRowDetail`), and any subscription wiring are untouched; `content-visibility: auto` only skips paint/layout for off-screen rows, leaving reactivity intact.
